videos.title: The 4 Year Cycle Is DEAD!! What It Means For Crypto In 2026!!
videos.text: Everyone was waiting for it. The blowoff top, the face melting parabolic rally that was supposed to send Bitcoin to $200,000 by Christmas 2025. We were promised a super cycle. We were promised the God candle. Instead, we got a 30% crash, extreme fear, and a lump of coal in our stockings. If you're looking at your portfolio right now wondering where it all went wrong, you're not alone. The Q4 explosion didn't happen. So, does this mean the famous four-year cycle is finally dead? Has the having lost its magic? Or has the market just evolved into something entirely different? Something that requires a completely new strategy if you want to survive 2026. Today, we're going to find out. We're going to look at why the cycle broke, why the smart money is actually targeting 2026, and exactly how you need to adjust your exit plan right now. You might want to take notes for this one. My name is Louis and you are watching the Coin Bureau. Now, before we break down the new timeline, we need to do a little housekeeping. First off, I am not a financial adviser and nothing in this video is financial advice. We are in uncharted territory here, folks. So, please do your own research. And secondly, if you appreciate us digging through the institutional data, so you don't have to prove it by punching that like button. It really does help the algorithm find other confused investors who need to see this. Done. Well, awesome. Let's get into it. Let's start with the cold, hard reality of where we stand today. At the time of shooting this video, it is December 30th, 2025, and Bitcoin is trading around $87,000. Now, 87K might sound great if you bought back in 2023, but context is everything. We are down over 30% from the all-time high of roughly $126,000 set back on October 6th. More importantly, Q4, which is historically the most bullish quarter for crypto, well, it has been a disaster. Bitcoin has registered a loss of nearly 24% this quarter. To put that in perspective, the last time we saw a Q4 this bad was in 2018, right in the depths of the bare market. Logically, this shouldn't be happening. The four-year cycle model, which has guided us faithfully for over a decade, predicted a peak in late 2025. In 2013, we peaked in November. In 2017, we peaked in December. In 2021, we peaked in November again. So, everyone positioned themselves for a November or December top in 2025. But the market, as it loves to do, humbled the consensus. Instead, we peaked in October, way earlier than the historical average suggests that we should have. So, what happened? Why did the post having explosion fizzle out? Well, the primary suspect is sitting right there on Wall Street. ETFs. Since their launch in early 2024, the spot Bitcoin ETFs have sucked up over $100 billion in assets. This has been incredible for price appreciation, sure, but it's come with a hidden cost. the death of volatility. Back in 2012, Bitcoin's volatility was over 200%. Today, it's hovering around 50%. The ETF effect has essentially smoothed out the cycle. Think about it. In previous cycles, retail investors like you and me drove the price. But retail is emotional. Retail FOMOs in all at once, creating that vertical god candle, and then panic sells all at once, creating an 80% crash. But now institutions hold nearly a quarter of the supply. Pension funds and corporate treasuries don't FOMO into a green candle on a Friday night. They rebalance quarterly. They buy when the price dips and sell when it rips to maintain a target allocation. This constant passive flow acts as a dampener. It caps the upside, preventing that insane blowoff top we were all waiting for. But here is the good news. It also cushions the downside. Notice how we haven't dropped 70% but instead stopped at 30%. That is the ETF bid wall in action. But the ETFs aren't the only culprit. We also have to talk about the macro disconnect. Historically, Bitcoin bull runs coincide with the Federal Reserve printing money. Loose money equals number go up. But 2025 was weird. For most of this year, the Fed was actually tightening, pulling liquidity out of the system through quantitative tightening or QT. Real yields were high and the cost of money was high. Bitcoin rallied to 126,000 despite the Fed trying to slow things down, largely thanks to that institutional demand that we just mentioned, but you could only swim upstream for so long. And by October, the liquidity drought finally caught up with the market. The Santa Alley was cancelled because there simply wasn't enough easy money slloshing around to fund it. However, just when it seems like the bears have won, here's the twist. On December 1st, the Fed officially ended QT. They have stopped draining liquidity. And history tells us that the end of QT is usually a precursor to the next phase, liquidity expansion. And this brings us to the most important part of this video, the new year timeline. If the four-year cycle based on the having is indeed broken, what replaces it? The answer is the liquidity cycle. If you overlay Bitcoin's price with global M2, that is the measure of all of the money in the world, correlation is pretty accurate. And according to global liquidity models, the cycle didn't peak in 2025. It is instead projected to peak in mid 2026. And this aligns with what we are hearing from some of the biggest names in the space. Tom Lee from Fundstrat is predicting a super cycle where Bitcoin grinds up to $200,000 throughout 2026. He argues that because the cycle started earlier, remember that preh havinging all-time high in March 2024, it is going to last longer but be less explosive. It's a left translated start with a right translated finish. I know, I know, technical jargon. Simply put, the party started up early, took a nap in late 2025, and is waking up for the afterparty in 2026. Even the big banks are on board. City Bank, Bernstein, Standard, Chartered, they are all forecasting the real peak to land between 150K and 200K next year. Gayscale is calling this the dawn of the institutional era. Basically, we are moving from a boom and bust cycle to a grind and hold cycle. So, what does this mean for you? For me, me. If you're still holding bags, waiting for a 2025 miracle, well, the market has changed and your exit plan might need to change with it. Here is the reality check. If you are waiting for a blowoff top, a day where Bitcoin goes up 20% in 24 hours and your altcoins do a 100x, well, you might be waiting forever. With volatility dampening, that signal might never come. Instead, we need to look at different indicators. First, forget the calendar. Watch the liquidity. Keep an eye on the Fed's balance sheet in Global M2. As long as those are expanding in early 2026, the trend is your friend. Second, look at onchain metrics like the MVRVZ score. Historically, cycle tops happen when this score hits the red zone, usually above five. Right now, we are sitting near two. That suggests that we are in a midcycle correction, not a cycle ending crash. And third, and most importantly, stop trying to time the absolute top. In this new lower volatility environment, the DCA out strategy is king. Instead of selling everything at a specific price target, you could sell small percentages as we grind up. This protects you if the super cycle turns out to be a super trap, but keeps you in the game if we do run to 200K. Not financial advice, of course. Look, I know that this Q4 has been painful. Seeing your portfolio bleed out during the holidays is never fun, but zooming out, the fundamental thesis hasn't broken. The having supply shock is real. It's just being digested differently by Wall Street than it was by crypto dgens. The demand also is real. Black Rocks ETF buyers didn't buy $60 billion worth of Bitcoin to dump it for a loss. And the macro winds are shifting in our favor as central banks prepare to turn the money printers back on in 2026. The four-year cycle as we knew it, the predictable clockwork boom and bust probably is dead, but it has been replaced by something potentially much more powerful. A mature liquiditydriven secular bull market. The easy money of the golden bull run might be over, but the smart money is just getting started. Now, I want to hear from you. Do you think the cycle is dead or are we just taking a breather before 2026? Are you buying this dip or are you packing your bags? Let me know in the comments below. And if you found this reality check useful, remember to smash that like button and subscribe to the Coin Bureau for more datadriven crypto content. That's going to be it for today. So, thank you all for watching and I'll see you again very soon. This Lewis signing off.

videos.title: What the Crash in Bitcoin Means Now (and for rest of 2026)
videos.text: All right, guys. Hello, welcome. In this particular video, I want to talk about what's been happening to the chart of Bitcoin. So, I'm going to discuss with you the chart picture overall for Bitcoin this year. And also importantly, I'm joined here with my special guest analyst Manuel Blay of the Dow Theory.com. And I'm going to get his view about the chart of Bitcoin as well. Should be quite interesting. Before I start though, some of you are probably wondering why I have not posted a video on YouTube for some time. The reason is I've been extremely busy with a lot of personal matters, which I can't discuss here right now, but I'll tell you more about that next month in July. Okay, let's go on to the chart of Bitcoin and let's bring in Manuel Blay of the Dow Theory here. Manuel, thanks very much indeed for joining me. So, how are you by the way? You well? >> I'm fine. I'm very honored to be your guest. >> Very kind of you. I appreciate it. By the way, just to give some context here, I know that for example, back in April when I the last time I had Manuel Blay here, so Manuel mentioned that we had a bullish signal triggered on Bitcoin back I think it occurred in April. And yeah, and how come Bitcoin and Ethereum could not maintain themselves in an uptrend and they fell. So, we'll get his view in a minute. But let me just mention guys that if we look at the weekly chart of Bitcoin, which I'll superimpose here so you can see it, you will see that Bitcoin actually got rejected from its weekly averages, from the 21 weekly moving average. Something I mentioned by the way in the member videos. By the way, you probably know that in the past in the videos I put out there on YouTube, I've mentioned that when price gets rejected from these weekly averages, especially weekly 21 and the weekly 34, it typically is a sign of a warning sign that something is not quite right. And by the way, in the member videos, I mentioned if Bitcoin were to trigger a break below the trend line support and 70,512, that could significantly increase downside risk to new lows. if Bitcoin were to drop down in a sustained way and breaks this support here 70,500 which is also the trend line support. That actually could increase downside risk quite significantly. If we're to see a sustained break of the support at 70,500 that actually could mean that Bitcoin is getting ready for a sustained decline, in other words, a strong drop and potential risk of retesting this prior low of February and potentially even taking that level out and falling below 60k. By breaking the 70,512 level and the trend line, it has increased the likelihood that what we had so far was a corrective rally, potentially ABC rally, up to this resistance region here and now breaking the support likely showing that Bitcoin getting rejected from resistance and the odds and the risk increasing for a potential decline and drop to take out this prior low. Again, the lows of February. All right, guys, thanks for watching that. Let's go back to our discussion with Manuel Blay. Manuel, so what happened? I mean, usually after a Dow Theory bullish signal there usually is more follow-through to the upside and usually a bull market then resumes or indeed starts. Except it appears the one that occurred in April of this year seems to have been a false signal. Is that correct? >> Uh yes, it was a false signal. And I do remember for our members that in that video I said that there was lots of technical damage. So, it was a bull signal but I remember I was skeptical as to its reaching really lofty levels. I said, "Look, because there is lots of what? Technical damage or of res- of resistance above the breakout levels." >> By the way, Manuel, you actually made a very interesting point in the member video which we just did, which is that about Ethereum being a strong confirmation leading indicator. Could you talk a very briefly about that? >> Very briefly, it means that when Bitcoin makes a higher high and Ethereum does not confirm, so Ethereum is not making a higher high, this is a yellow flag. It doesn't mean a bear market, but it means that the higher high in Bitcoin is suspect. And this happened now in May, if you look at the charts for several days, I think three, four days Bitcoin continued making a higher highs while Ethereum did not. This also happened in January 2026 this year when many people thought that the the reaction was the end of the bear market and Bitcoin make made a higher high and Ethereum did not and you know what followed afterwards, the big dive, the big plunge. And it also happened, very importantly, in October 2025 when Bitcoin made an all-time high and Ethereum did not. Was this a bear market signal? No, but it was another a warning sign. And this is precisely the warning sign we got now in May this year with Bitcoin. And this is why in my latest crypto report in June 1st, I said, "A bull market under probation." Because I said, "Look, if Bitcoin does not reach, confirmed by Ethereum, this higher level, then the bull market is suspect and suspect it was." >> To be honest, I was hoping the uptrend would continue because usually I've seen when a Dow theory bullish signal occurs, I was expecting and I was hoping that the uptrend would continue into the '80s, you know, the mid '80s and higher levels. I wasn't expecting Bitcoin to break down that trendline support, but when it did, we had no choice but to change our mind and as I mentioned in the memo >> No questions asked. >> Exactly, no questions asked. Let's take a look at the charts. All right, let's dig into it. So, Manuel, as we can see here on the weekly chart, so there is that trendline break that occurred on the right-hand side. We've reached another 200-weekly moving average there at the green line on the chart. And we look at the monthly chart, something is interesting to me is the monthly 50 has now been reached as well. Uh now it doesn't mean it's going to hold those levels. I mean as we know, nothing is guaranteed here, but it is interesting that those levels of support have now been tested, the monthly 50, and the weekly 200. But overall, where do you think Bitcoin may head to in the next several weeks and months, assuming we stay below resistance? >> It could well go down to 50,000, 49,000 level. Look at the dotted gray line you have >> Yes, yes. >> that is connecting the July, August 20 2025 lows. And this could This is a logical support on the chart. So it could go down there. >> Yeah, yeah. And >> For me, this is the next the next target. >> So this is the level >> Exactly. Yeah. >> talking about there, so >> Yes. >> Just under 50K, 49K. Uh so that's that's your next potential key level. >> Yeah, it's interesting. And I And I think you have a point there. So Manuel, what about the daily chart here? We so it is quite a bit oversold. For example, on some on some key measurements, it seems that Bitcoin has fallen to oversold conditions, and it's also taken out just near 60K. Do you think some kind of a bounce is likely? We haven't got it just yet, but do you think perhaps some kind of a short-term recovery bounce is probable in the next several days? >> be it would be a dead cat bounce. I think all the oscillators are deeply oversold right now, so it's possible, but don't make no mistake about it. It would be a relief rally, a dead cat bounce. The trend is bearish. So furthermore, if you couple this with the trend of gold, the trend of the stock market is it has not changed, but we see bearishness all around, which means tight liquidity. So this is a headwind against Bitcoin. So we can get a a uh a small bounce. Yes, it's normal. It's oversold, but is a change of trend? No. The trend is Ethereum is confirming and furthermore, Ethereum is also confirming now the bearish action, which is This is bearish. And furthermore, if you look at the ratios, uh Bitcoin against gold, gold is falling, but it's falling less than Bitcoin, which is bearish for Bitcoin. Because if Bitcoin is going to be digital gold, then Bitcoin should be stronger than gold. >> So, we can see that Bitcoin is significantly underperforming gold. So, >> average, the green one. I have I guess it's a 200-day >> 200, yes. 200 is sloping downwards, yes. >> Yeah, yeah, like like my charts. I I pay lots of attention to the 200-day moving average and look, it's ominous and this is why when it was a bull market, I said it was under probation because this ratio is not good. And for Bitcoin to shine, to really be strong, it needs to be stronger than gold. Because if digital gold, Bitcoin, is going to be the gold of the future, then it must be stronger than gold. And furthermore, if you if we zoom or >> Yeah. >> So, if we take a longer perspective of the ratio, we will see that when Bitcoin in the past was really strong, it was the ratio was bullish for Bitcoin. So, Bitcoin Look. Look. >> You see? >> Yeah, you're right. In the past, >> was in in the past was really strong and making higher highs, the ratio not only the 200-day moving average, the green the the green line, >> was trending upwards. And and now, it's ominous, but it's really trending downwards. So, it means that even though gold has fallen, it has fallen less than Bitcoin. So, gold is stronger. So, physical gold is stronger than digital one. This is not good. >> So, we're looking at a chart of MicroStrategy divided by Bitcoin on this chart. So, what what do you what do you see in this chart? What is it telling you? >> I see a huge bearish action. Look, when the market for crypto, for Bitcoin, is risk on, MicroStrategy is the leverage play. So, MicroStrategy tends to outperform Bitcoin, and this is bullish for Bitcoin. When, on the other hand, the crypto space is risk off, then strategy becomes weaker than Bitcoin's. In this chart, it means that then the ratio goes under because Bitcoin falls, but falls less than strategy. And this is bearish for Bitcoin. So, yes, I'm falling less, but I'm falling. And it means a risk-off environment. But this is bearish for Bitcoin. >> It's worth mentioning just something interesting and important here, which is this particular rally that we're seeing here. If we measure that, and then we project that from this low, the 100% projection of that, so the symmetrical 100% projection, was this red level. So, that 80,906 level here, or 81K, was a level essentially that Bitcoin held. Bitcoin held this level, this resistance, and also at the the 200-day moving average here, and then dropped below support, so some kind of a a larger degree corrective pattern, potential A wave, B wave, and a likely C wave into resistance, and then breaking of support indicated a continuation of the bear market. So, I think it's just worth mentioning that at this point because it's it's always good to learn these points. Thank you very much, Manuel. Guys, make sure you check out Manuel Blay's excellent newsletter, which is the dowtheory.com. Manuel has provided you a very nice discount and offer, as you can see here. Go ahead and check it out.
